channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How one chip stock reversed the global tech selloff, exposed AI's 'memory tax' and made the case for an entire valuation regime change
item.description: Micron had a 'drop the mic' quarter, Dan Ives said. Others called it a restructuring of how Wall Street will price the AI trade for years to come.

item.title: Deviled eggs, seltzer and a burger you can't quit: The GLP-1 crowd is (halfway) reinventing the American BBQ
item.description: Price hikes, Ozempic culture, and a stubborn love of beef are colliding at the grill this July 4th.

item.title: Private equity gets cut of two of Taylor Swift's biggest pop hits through Max Martin's catalog sale
item.description: HarbourView's deal for select Max Martin and Shellback publishing rights doesn't touch Swift's masters.

item.title: GTA 6 release date finally confirmed, but $80 price and no disc anger gamers
item.description: Rockstar announced Grand Theft Auto VI launches Nov. 19 at $79.99, but initial boxes lack a physical disc, sparking criticism.
